# Composable Architecture

## The Physics

Complex systems are most robust and adaptable when built from simple,
interchangeable components with defined interfaces. Each component does one
thing well. Components connect through standard interfaces. New capabilities
emerge from new combinations of existing components, not from building
new monoliths.

This applies to governance as much as technology: governance objects are
components (concepts, principles, standards, rules) that compose into
governance systems through defined relationships.

## Why It Matters

- **Reuse**: Components designed once are used in many contexts.
- **Testing**: Simple components are individually testable. Monoliths are not.
- **Evolution**: Replace one component without rebuilding the system.
- **Understanding**: Small components are comprehensible. Monoliths are not.

## Academic/Industry Backing

- Unix philosophy: small, composable programs that do one thing well.
- Microservices architecture: bounded services with defined interfaces.
- LEGO metaphor: standard connection interface enables infinite combinations.
- Bill of Materials (manufacturing): assemblies built from cataloged components.

## Connected Objects

- JOS-P12 (Design Once, Compose Many) — the principle of composable design
- JOS-C01 (Fractal Self-Similarity) — composable patterns are fractal
- JOS-S01 (Discoverable Registries) — components must be discoverable to be composable

### Framework Connection

Framework §7 (template/instance model with Empty/Null/Filled states) and §8 (slot fill model) provide the concrete composition mechanism — components compose by filling slots in templates, making composability structural rather than ad-hoc.
